L1.area: User input validation and form submission processing

L2.description: An improper authentication vulnerability exists where a validation check on user-submitted challenge-response codes only verifies that the input is non-empty rather than verifying it matches the expected format and length, allowing attackers to potentially bypass the verification mechanism with crafted input.

L3.description: An improper authentication vulnerability exists in the CAPTCHA validation logic where the sanity check on user-submitted CAPTCHA codes only verifies that the input is non-empty rather than verifying the input length matches the expected CAPTCHA code length, potentially allowing attackers to bypass CAPTCHA verification with incorrect-length input.

ground_truth.reason: The validateCaptchaCode function only checked if the submitted CAPTCHA code was empty (length === 0), instead of verifying that the code length matched the expected CAPTCHA length, allowing potential bypass of CAPTCHA validation.
